title: The Bank of New York is founded
commonsFile: File:Alexander Hamilton portrait by John Trumbull 1806.jpg
note: Current image is the modern Federal Reserve Bank logo; Hamilton wrote the bank's constitution, and this is a genuine period portrait.

title: New York becomes the national capital
commonsFile: File:Federal Hall, N.Y. 1789 ppmsca.15703.jpg
note: Current is a modern photo of Albany; this is a period engraving of Federal Hall, the seat of Congress.

title: The African Free School opens
note: Genuine period engraving of the African Free School.

title: Washington inaugurated at Federal Hall
note: 19th-century history painting (Elorriaga) of the 1789 inauguration; period-appropriate.

title: Congress proposes the Bill of Rights
note: Image of the original Bill of Rights document.

title: The federal capital moves to Philadelphia
commonsFile: File:Congress Hall & New Theatre Birch's Views Plate 20.jpg
note: Current is a modern New York state seal; this is an 1800 Birch engraving of Congress Hall, Philadelphia.

title: The Buttonwood Agreement founds the NYSE
note: Topical Buttonwood Agreement image, not a modern building photo.

title: The worst yellow fever epidemic
note: Period Birch engraving from the yellow-fever era; genuine early-1800s image.

title: New York passes gradual abolition
note: Image of the actual Gradual Abolition act.

title: Yellow fever returns
note: Current is a modern Manhattan skyline photo; no suitable NYC period image for the 1803 epidemic.

title: The New-York Historical Society is founded
commonsFile: File:John Pintard, by John Ramage (cropped).jpg
note: Current is a modern photo of the building; this is a period portrait of founder John Pintard.

title: Fulton's steamboat sails the Hudson
note: Period portrait of Robert Fulton (Benjamin West, 1806).

title: The Commissioners' Plan grids Manhattan
commonsFile: File:Commissioners' Plan of 1811 by John Randel published in 1821.jpg
note: Current is a modern comparison SVG; this is the actual 1811 Commissioners' Plan map by John Randel.

title: Harbor forts rise before the War of 1812
commonsFile: File:Castle garden, New York- From the battery LCCN90714333.jpg
note: Current is a 20th-century HABS photo; this is a period lithograph of the Battery fort (Castle Garden/West Battery).

title: Fulton's steam ferry links Brooklyn
note: Genuine 19th-century photograph of a Fulton Ferry vessel.

title: Gas lamps light up Broadway
note: Current image is an unrelated Benjamin West painting of Franklin drawing electricity; no fitting period Broadway gaslight image.

title: The Erie Canal opens
commonsFile: File:Grand Canal Celebration- View of the Fleet Preparing to Form in Line MET DR132.jpg
note: Current is a modern photo; this is the 1825 Anthony Imbert aquatint of the Erie Canal opening celebration.

title: Delmonico's opens
commonsFile: File:Supper at Delmonico's, New York 1898.jpg
note: Current is a modern photo of the building; this is a period image of Delmonico's.

title: Slavery ends in New York State
note: Current is a 1917 Howard Pyle illustration of a 1655 slave auction — not period and thematically wrong for the 1827 emancipation.

title: New York University is founded
commonsFile: File:New York University Building in Washington Square, 1850.jpg
note: Current is a photo of a Gallatin statue; this is a period engraving of NYU's original University Building.

title: Cholera devastates the city
note: Current is a modern High Bridge photo; no suitable genuine NYC period image of the 1832 cholera epidemic located.

title: The city's first railroad begins running
note: Current is a modern subway car; no verified period image of the 1832 New York & Harlem horsecar located.

title: The Great Fire destroys Lower Manhattan
note: Period 1835 lithograph of the Great Fire.

title: The Panic of 1837 hits Wall Street
note: Period 1837 political cartoon (The Times).

title: Horace Greeley founds the New-York Tribune
note: Period (1864) image of the Tribune.

title: Barnum opens his American Museum
note: Period photograph of Barnum's American Museum (William England).

title: Croton water reaches Manhattan
note: Period photograph of High Bridge on the Croton Aqueduct (William England).

title: Famine Irish transform the city
commonsFile: File:Emigrants Leave Ireland by Henry Doyle 1868.jpg
note: Current is a modern skyline photo; this is an 1868 period engraving of Irish emigration.

title: The Astor Place Riot turns deadly
note: Period lithograph of the 1849 Astor Place riot.

title: Cholera strikes again
note: Current image is a random modern Yemen airstrikes SVG; no suitable period image for the 1849 cholera epidemic located.

title: The Crystal Palace hosts America's first world's fair
note: Period 1853 lithograph of the New York Crystal Palace.

title: The state sets aside land for Central Park
commonsFile: File:Viele's Survey for Central Park.jpg
note: Current is a modern flag SVG; this is Egbert Viele's 1856 period survey map of the future Central Park land.

title: Castle Garden becomes an immigrant depot
commonsFile: File:Immigrants at Castle Garden, New York City, 1866.jpg
note: Current is a 20th-century HABS photo; this is a period image of immigrants at the Castle Garden depot.

title: The Panic of 1857 rattles New York
note: Period image of the 1857 bank panic.

title: Two police forces battle in the streets
note: Period engraving of the 1857 New York police riot.

title: The Greensward Plan wins Central Park
commonsFile: File:Central Park, Winter- The skating pond LCCN90715719.jpg
note: Current is the 1639 Manatus map (wrong era); this is a Currier & Ives period view of the newly designed Central Park.

title: Peter Cooper founds Cooper Union
commonsFile: File:Peter Cooper daguerreotype.png
note: Current is a modern photo of the building; this is a period daguerreotype portrait of founder Peter Cooper.

title: Lincoln speaks at Cooper Union
commonsFile: File:Abraham Lincoln, candidate for U.S. president, before delivering his Cooper Union address, New York, N.Y., on Feb. 27, 1860 LCCN2009630664.jpg
note: Current is a modern building photo; this is the Mathew Brady portrait taken the day of the Cooper Union address.

title: The Draft Riots convulse the city
note: Period Harper's Weekly engraving of the 1863 Draft Riots.

title: A professional fire department is born
commonsFile: File:The Life of a Fireman - Currier and Ives.png
note: Current is a random modern photo; this is a period Currier & Ives firefighting lithograph.

title: The Metropolitan Health Act reforms sanitation
note: Current is an unrelated photo of an Iranian qanat; no fitting genuine period image located.

title: Prospect Park opens in Brooklyn
commonsFile: File:1868 Vaux and Olmstead Map of Prospect Park, Brooklyn, New York - Geographicus - ProspectPark-bishop-1868.jpg
note: Current is a modern photo; this is the period 1868 Olmsted and Vaux map of Prospect Park.

title: The first elevated railway opens
note: Period print of the elevated railway.

title: The Museum of Natural History is founded
commonsFile: File:Albert Smith Bickmore.png
note: Current is a modern photo of the museum; this is a period portrait of founder Albert Bickmore.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art is founded
note: Current is a modern photo of the museum; no verified period image of the 1870 founding located.

title: The Tweed Ring is exposed
note: Period photograph of Thomas Nast, whose cartoons exposed the ring.

title: The Panic of 1873 shuts the exchange
note: Period engraving of the 1873 bank run.

title: Boss Tweed is convicted
note: Period photograph of William M. Tweed.

title: The Natural History Museum opens its home
commonsFile: File:American Museum of Natural History, 1917.jpg
note: Current is a present-day photo; this is an early-20th-century period photograph of the historic museum building.

title: Elevated railroads spread across Manhattan
note: Period print of the Manhattan elevated railway.

title: The dumbbell tenement law
note: Period (c.1900) photograph of a dumbbell-tenement airshaft.

title: The Met opens on Fifth Avenue
note: Current is a modern photo; no verified period image of the 1880 Vaux and Mould building located.

title: Edison lights up Lower Manhattan
note: Period image of Edison's Pearl Street Station.

title: The Brooklyn Bridge opens
commonsFile: File:Currier and Ives, The Great East River Suspension Bridge - Connecting the Cities of New York and Brooklyn, 1881, NGA 185010.jpg
note: Current is a modern skyline photo; this is the Currier & Ives lithograph of the bridge.

title: The Metropolitan Opera opens
note: Period photograph of the 39th Street Metropolitan Opera House.

title: The Statue of Liberty is dedicated
commonsFile: File:EdwardMoran-UnveilingTheStatueofLiberty1886Large.jpg
note: Current is a modern photo; this is Edward Moran's 1886 painting of the statue's dedication.

title: The Great Blizzard of 1888
note: Period weather map of the 1888 blizzard.

title: Riis publishes How the Other Half Lives
note: Period cover of the 1890 book.

title: The New York Botanical Garden is founded
commonsFile: File:Nathaniel Lord Britton.jpg
note: Current is a modern photo of the visitor center; this is a period portrait of founder Nathaniel Lord Britton.

title: Carnegie Hall opens
commonsFile: File:Carnegie Hall exterior, 1899.jpg
note: Current is a modern photo; this is an 1899 period photograph of Carnegie Hall's exterior.

title: Ellis Island opens
commonsFile: File:Original building at Ellis Island 1892. (bcdcc58c-155d-451f-6736-9c2f0585ab2a).jpg
note: Current is a modern aerial photo; this is a period photograph of the original 1892 Ellis Island station.

title: A cholera scare shuts the harbor
note: Period photograph (Koch's laboratory) tied to the 1881–1896 cholera pandemic.

title: The Rapid Transit Act plans a subway
commonsFile: File:Beach Pneumatic Transit 01.jpg
note: Current is a modern subway-map SVG; this is a period engraving of Beach's pneumatic transit, referenced in the event.

title: Voters approve consolidation
note: Period 1897 map of the consolidating City of Greater New York.

title: Waring's "White Wings" clean the streets
note: Period photograph of street-cleaning commissioner George Waring.
